Title: Bifunctional CYP81AA proteins catalyse identical hydroxylations but alternative regioselective phenol couplings in plant xanthone biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NADPH-cytochrome P450 reductase, class II CPR
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by yeast-extract elicitation in H. calycinum cell cultures; transcript levels increased from 4 h and peaked at 8 h post elicitation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In H. calycinum cell cultures, elicitor inducible with peak transcript accumulation at 8 h post elicitation; expressed in yeast microsomes for functional support
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL promoters of pESC-URA for yeast co-expression
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Yeast extract elicitation (3 g l−1) of H. calycinum cell cultures; NADPH-dependent microsomal assays
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: H. calycinum elicited cell cultures accumulate hyperxanthone E, a 1,3,7-trihydroxyxanthone derivative
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense compounds against herbivores and microorganisms; xanthones also have human pharmacological activities including anti-Alzheimer properties
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides electron transfer required for CYP81AA catalytic formation of xanthone products in yeast microsomes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HcCPR2 was cloned as a full-length class II CPR from elicitor-treated H. calycinum cell cultures. 2. Recombinant HcCPR2 transferred electrons from NADPH to cytochrome c and supported CYP81AA activities in yeast microsomes. 3. Its elicitor-induced expression paralleled HcBPS and HcCYP81AA1 before hyperxanthone E accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA cloning; heterologous co-expression in yeast; microsome preparation; cytochrome c reduction assay; RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as electron-transfer partner for HcCYP81AA1, HpCYP81AA1, HpCYP81AA2 and HpCYP81AA3 in yeast functional assays; not itself a xanthone pathway catalyst for carbon skeleton formation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful support component for heterologous reconstruction of xanthone-pathway CYP reactions in yeast or other engineering platforms
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Suppression subtractive hybridization cDNA library of elicitor-treated H. calycinum cell cultures

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Cytochrome P450 CYP81AA1 / bifunctional benzophenone 3'-hydroxylase and 1,3,7-trihydroxyxanthone synthase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses consecutive 3'-hydroxylation of 2,4,6-trihydroxybenzophenone to 2,3',4,6-tetrahydroxybenzophenone and intramolecular C–O phenol coupling of the latter to form 1,3,7-trihydroxyxanthone
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-determining step; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced by yeast extract in H. calycinum cell cultures; transcript levels rose from 4 h and peaked at 8 h post elicitation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In H. calycinum cell cultures, expression precedes hyperxanthone E accumulation; in yeast microsomes produces xanthone products
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL promoters of pESC-URA for yeast expression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Yeast extract elicitation of H. calycinum cell cultures; NADPH-dependent microsomal assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Elicited H. calycinum cell cultures accumulate hyperxanthone E, a 1,3,7-trihydroxyxanthone derivative
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense metabolite precursor in plants; xanthones are bioactive natural products with pharmacological potential
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of 1,3,7-trihydroxyxanthone and thereby channels flux into the 1,3,7-trihydroxyxanthone-derived xanthone branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HcCYP81AA1 was cloned from elicitor-treated H. calycinum cell cultures as a new CYP81AA subfamily member. 2. In yeast microsomes it converted 2,3',4,6-tetraHB to 1,3,7-triHX and converted 2,4,6-triHB to both 2,3',4,6-tetraHB and 1,3,7-triHX. 3. Expression of HcCYP81AA1 was inducible by yeast extract and temporally preceded hyperxanthone E accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA cloning; heterologous co-expression with HcCPR2 in yeast; microsomal enzyme assays; HPLC-DAD; LC-MS/MS; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Bifunctional enzyme with both monooxygenase and oxidative phenol-coupling activities; also converted maclurin to 1,3,6,7-tetrahydroxyxanthone; did not accept several other tested benzophenones/xanthones
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential catalyst for selective engineering of the 1,3,7-trihydroxyxanthone scaffold in heterologous systems or medicinal plant metabolic engineering
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Suppression subtractive hybridization cDNA library of elicitor-treated H. calycinum cell cultures

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Cytochrome P450 CYP81AA1 / bifunctional benzophenone 3'-hydroxylase and 1,3,7-trihydroxyxanthone synthase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Orthologous bifunctional CYP that catalyses 3'-hydroxylation of 2,4,6-trihydroxybenzophenone and para-oriented intramolecular C–O phenol coupling of 2,3',4,6-tetrahydroxybenzophenone to form 1,3,7-trihydroxyxanthone
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-determining step; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferential expression in roots and leaves according to MPGR FPKM values
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In yeast microsomes activity resembled HcCYP81AA1
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL promoters of pESC-URA for yeast expression
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: H. perforatum synthesizes both 1,3,7- and 1,3,5-trihydroxyxanthone derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related xanthone precursor with pharmacological relevance of downstream derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of 1,3,7-trihydroxyxanthone in H. perforatum xanthone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. H. perforatum locus 416 encoded a CYP highly similar to HcCYP81AA1. 2. Its yeast-expressed enzyme activity profile resembled HcCYP81AA1 and identified it as HpCYP81AA1. 3. It represents the H. perforatum orthologue of 1,3,7-TXS.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome mining; cDNA cloning; heterologous co-expression with HcCPR2 in yeast; microsomal substrate assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Differs from HcCYP81AA1 by only 24 amino acids
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate catalyst for production of 1,3,7-trihydroxyxanthone-derived medicinal xanthones
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public H. perforatum transcriptomes in MPGR; FPKM expression data

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Cytochrome P450 CYP81AA2 / bifunctional benzophenone 3'-hydroxylase and 1,3,5-trihydroxyxanthone synthase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses 3'-hydroxylation of 2,4,6-trihydroxybenzophenone and ortho-oriented intramolecular C–O phenol coupling of 2,3',4,6-tetrahydroxybenzophenone to form predominantly 1,3,5-trihydroxyxanthone
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-determining step; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferential expression in roots and leaves according to MPGR FPKM values
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In yeast microsomes forms mainly 1,3,5-triHX from tetraHB
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL promoters of pESC-URA for yeast expression
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related xanthone precursor with pharmaceutical potential in downstream derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of 1,3,5-trihydroxyxanthone and directs flux into the 1,3,5-trihydroxyxanthone branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. H. perforatum locus 928 encoded the paralogous enzyme HpCYP81AA2. 2. In yeast microsomes it converted 2,3',4,6-tetraHB mainly to 1,3,5-triHX, with minor 1,3,7-triHX and one unidentified product. 3. Reciprocal mutagenesis identified S375, L378 and A483 as major determinants of regioselectivity, and a sextuple mutant shifted product output strongly toward 1,3,7-triHX.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome mining; cDNA cloning; heterologous co-expression with HcCPR2 in yeast; microsomal substrate assays; HPLC-DAD; LC-MS/MS; homology modelling; site-directed mutagenesis; product docking
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Bifunctional enzyme; wild type produced ~79.5% 1,3,5-triHX and ~11.0% 1,3,7-triHX from 2,3',4,6-tetraHB; unexpectedly formed 1,3,6,7-tetrahydroxyxanthone from maclurin rather than 1,3,5,6-tetrahydroxyxanthone
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Attractive catalyst for selective production of 1,3,5-trihydroxyxanthone; identified residues enable rational engineering of xanthone scaffold regioselectivity
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public H. perforatum transcriptomes in MPGR; FPKM expression data

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses ring closure of maclurin to 1,3,6,7-tetrahydroxyxanthone
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an alternative xanthone product outside the main trihydroxyxanthone branch studied
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: More abundant in flower organs according to MPGR FPKM values
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In yeast microsomes only the maclurin-to-1,3,6,7-tetrahydroxyxanthone reaction was observed
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL promoters of pESC-URA for yeast expression
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Xanthone scaffold with pharmacological relevance of downstream derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Forms 1,3,6,7-tetrahydroxyxanthone from maclurin; physiological role remained unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. H. perforatum locus 8128 encoded HpCYP81AA3. 2. The only detected activity was formation of 1,3,6,7-tetrahydroxyxanthone from maclurin. 3. Its physiological function in planta was not resolved.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome mining; cDNA cloning; heterologous co-expression with HcCPR2 in yeast; microsomal substrate assays
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally characterized in the Results, though the main paper focus is on CYP81AA1 and CYP81AA2; not assigned as 1,3,5- or 1,3,7-TXS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tool for alternative xanthone scaffold synthesis, but biological deployment requires further clarification
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public H. perforatum transcriptomes in MPGR; FPKM expression data
